LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANTS GEORGES NAASAN AND THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED IN PART AND TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION IN PART ON OCT-06-2017 BY HAROLD E. KAHN. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DISPARATE IMPACT CLAIM. COURT STATES FINDINGS AND REASONS IN OPEN FORUM. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS OTHERWISE TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION AS TO ALL OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION AS OF THIS DATE BY JUDGE HAROLD KAHN. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: GENA EALES, CSR NO. 9176, E-MAIL: GEALES@SFTC.ORG. (L&M302)

LAW AND MOTION 302, SOME PARTS PREVIOULY TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION ON OCTOBER 6, 2017, DEFENDANTS GEORGES NAASAN AND THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DISPARATE IMPACT DISABILITY DISCRIMINATION, THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FAILURE TO ENGAGE IN THE INTERACTIVE PROCESS, AND THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FAILURE TO ACCOMMODATE AND DENIED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DISPARATE TREATMENT DISABILITY DISCRIMINATION AND THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR RETALIATION. COUNSEL FOR DR. HAGEN IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH IS CONSISTENT WITH THE MY RULINGS AND COMPLIES WITH CCP 437C(G) AND SUBMIT IT TO COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANTS FOR REVIEW. IF PARTIES CANNTO AGREE ON A PROPOSED ORDER, THEY SHOULD SUBMIT COMPETING ORDERS. (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Exhibit H To The Declaration Of Jayne Hagen, Ph.D. In Support Of Plaintiffs Opposition To Defendants Motion For Summary Adjudication; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Emily S. Mcgrath
Off Calendar Oct-18-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JAYNE HAGEN PH.D's Motion To Seal Exhibit H To The Declaration Of Jayne Hagen, Ph.D. In Support Of Plaintiffs Opposition To Defendants Motion For Summary Adjudication.

Plaintiff Jayne Hagen's motion to seal Exhibit H to the declaration of Jayne Hagen in opposition to defendants' motion for summary adjudication is granted. No opposition filed and good cause shown. Moving party to submit a proposed order which complies with CRC 2.550(e). Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Gordon Aiavao, CSR No: 11216, email: gaiavao@sftc.org. (D302)

*The proposed order submitted by email is rejected. (see notes.)*

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION .
Off Calendar Oct-26-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Barbara A. Lawless and Emily S. McGrath of the law offices of Lawless & Lawless (415-391-7555) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Delia A. Isvoranu of the law offices of Sedgwick LLP (415-781-7900) appears as counsel for defendants/moving parties

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

DEFENDANTS GEORGES NAASAN and THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is GRANTED in part and taken UNDER SUBMISSION in part. Summary adjudication is GRANTEd as to the 2nd Cause of action for disparate impact claim. Court states findings and reasons in open forum. The motion for summary adjudication is otherwise taken UNDER SUBMISSION as to all other causes of action as of this date by Judge Harold Kahn.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Gena Eales, CSR No. 9176, e-mail: geales@sftc.org. (L&M302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel A Response To Request For Production Number 46
Off Calendar Sep-25-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Emily s. McGrath, Esq. of Lawless & Lawless, Tel. 415.391.7555, for plaintiff and moving party;
No other appearance.

Plaintiff's counsel stipulated to the Judge Pro Tem.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF JAYNE HAGEN'S Motion To Compel A Response To Request For Production Number 46 as follows: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL A RESPONSE TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION NUMBER 46 IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS WILL PRODUCE WITHIN TEN (10) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER A REDACTED COPY OF ITS APEX RECORDS REQUESTED BY PLAINTIFF SUBJECT TO THE AGREED TO PROTECTIVE ORDER. NO SANCTIONS WERE SOUGHT AND NONE WILL BE IMPOSED. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.
